_comment: Declarative workflow definition for skill-repair. Applies an approved skill-health repair plan by separating mechanical (safe to automate) from judgment (human-only) fixes, printing an approval summary, and on confirmation applying ONLY the safe mechanical fixes then re-running the drift guard.
description: Approved repair: read plan → separate mechanical/judgment → approval summary → apply mechanical only → drift guard → quality gate

steps.note: Require an approved plan from skill-health. If none, produce a short diagnosis + proposed changes and STOP for approval first.
Tag each item: mechanical (regenerate derived files, re-run drift guard) or judgment (design change, logic edit, refactor). Judgment items STAY with the human.
Plain-language summary: what will change, why, what stays the same, any risk. STOP for human approval before applying.
Dry-run by default. --apply performs ONLY safe mechanical fixes. Judgment items are left as TODOs for the human.
Must exit 0 after every repair. If drift detected, report and stop.

note: Tight scope: smallest stable fix, preserve untouched behavior, explicit approval trail. Never silently expand a narrow fix into a redesign.
